query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Were net outflows continuing into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026?** **Yes, but with a mid-week inflection.** That shortened week (July 3 was closed for US Independence Day) logged ~**–$527M net outflows** across June 29–July 2 — the **8th consecutive week of outflows** (cumulative >$8.2B over 8 weeks). However, the 10-day daily outflow streak (~$2.73B) **ended July 2** with the first positive day (+$221M). Flows then turned decisively positive in early July (+$510M over July 6–8).

**Context:** June 2026 was the **worst outflow month on record** for US spot BTC ETFs (~$4.06–4.5B), with BlackRock's IBIT driving ~73% of late-June selling.

**Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026:** Clear **downtrend** — BTC fell ~9% from ~$64,240 (June 20) to a 3-month low of **$58,559 on June 30**, coinciding almost exactly with the peak of ETF outflows. It then recovered ~9% into early/mid-July (~$64,000 on July 6, ~$63,758 on July 12) as flows reversed positive. IBIT mirrored this, bottoming at $33.29 on June 30 before bouncing.

Full details, data points, and sources have been provided in the structured output.

key_facts: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded ~-$527M net outflows over June 29–July 2, 2026 (July 3 closed for US Independence Day), the 8th consecutive week of outflows.
The 10-day daily outflow streak (~$2.73B) ended July 2, 2026 with a first positive session of ~+$221–223M.
June 2026 was the worst outflow month on record for US spot BTC ETFs (~$4.06B to $4.5B), beating the prior record of -$3.56B (Feb 2025).
Week of June 22–26, 2026 saw -$1.79B outflows, with IBIT accounting for ~73% (-$1.30B); IBIT lost $444.5M on June 26 alone.
BlackRock's IBIT drove ~73% of late-June outflows and had an 11-session redemption streak (~$2.2B) ending July 6 with +$209.4M.
Flows reversed positive in early July: ~+$510M inflows over July 6–8, 2026.
Bitcoin fell ~9% from ~$64,240 (June 20 close) to a 3-month low of $58,559 on June 30, 2026, then recovered ~9% into mid-July.
BTC bottomed (June 30) almost exactly as the ETF outflow streak broke (July 2), then rose to ~$63,995 by July 6.
IBIT (largest ETF) fell to a recent low of $33.29 on June 30, then bounced ~8.8% to ~$36.23 by July 10.
The early-July reversal was linked to June jobs data cooling rate risk and renewed institutional buying.
